description: Constructs a Doob martingale over the sequence reveals. The increments depend on how one letter changes the expected LCS, allowing Azuma-Hoeffding to tightly bound the variance and mean.
analogical_connections: Updating your estimate of the final score in a game as each play occurs, with strict thermodynamic limits on how much one single play can change the expectation.
implementation_hypothesis: Compute the exact martingale differences for small n using dynamic programming, and use their empirical variance to tighten the McDiarmid inequality bound.
experiment_seed: Compute the conditional expectations E[LCS | x_1...x_k, y_1...y_k] exhaustively for n=15 to analyze the variance profile.
experimental_result: Hoeffding concentration bound successfully applied in our main result (concept 005). Key enabler.
